key_insight: A machine-learning pipeline combining imputation, imbalance handling, feature selection, and ensemble modeling was used to predict early Alzheimer’s disease and highlight potential biomarkers.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could support earlier identification of Alzheimer’s-related changes before symptoms become severe, helping guide timely clinical follow-up.
method: Used ADNI clinical, neuropsychological, and neuroimaging-related data.
Filled missing values with iterative imputation and addressed class imbalance with Borderline SVM-SMOTE.
Compared a stacking ensemble and an artificial neural network after feature selection and scaling.
evidence_signal: Model performance was evaluated using precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC on selected features from the ADNI dataset.
watch_item: The abstract does not report external validation, so real-world generalizability and biomarker robustness remain uncertain.
paper_title: Predicting Early Stages Of Alzheimer's Disease And Identifying Key Biomarkers Using Deep Artificial Neural Network And Ensemble Of Machine Learning Methodologies
